title: Candollea mollis
taxonomy: Candollea mollis belongs to the family Santalaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the sandalwood family. It is a genus of flowering plants that is native to Australia. The species Candollea mollis is a shrub or small tree that is found in eastern Australia. The classification of Candollea mollis includes the kingdom Plantae, and the order Santalales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaves and flowers.
uses: Candollea mollis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes, although its effectiveness and safety may not be supported by modern scientific evidence. Traditionally, Candollea mollis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
